content.full_text:
RAW MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
October 30,
1995
Jackie Goldberg
Councilmember,
13th District
City Hall
200 N. Spring Street,
Room 240.
Los Angeles,
CA 90012
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
1—95—286
Dear Ms. Goldberg:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act “the Act”.-/
Since your letter states
a general question,
we consider it to be a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).
2
?
You have asked if an elected official may form a nonprofit
organization.
Since you have not provided any facts concerning
the nonprofit organization,
we cannot provide, specific advice
pertaining to this issue.
Generally,
an elected official is not
precluded from forming a nonprofit organization, provided it is
formed in a manner consistent with Regulation 18217
(copy
enclosed).
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
-
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
by:
bixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660

sections.question: You have asked if an elected official may form a nonprofit organization.
sections.conclusion: Generally, an elected official is not precluded from forming a nonprofit organization, provided it is formed in a manner consistent with Regulation 18217.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an elected official may form a nonprofit organization under California's Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An elected official may form a nonprofit organization if it is formed in compliance with Regulation 18217. However, the FPPC notes they cannot provide specific advice without facts about the particular nonprofit organization.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document references Regulation 18217 as the applicable standard. Footnote 2 clarifies that informal assistance does not provide immunity like a formal opinion would.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked if an elected official may form a nonprofit organization.

CONCLUSION: Generally, an elected official is not precluded from forming a nonprofit organization, provided it is formed in a manner consistent with Regulation 18217.
embedding.first_500_words: RAW MEHTA CHAIRMAN FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION October 30, 1995 Jackie Goldberg Councilmember, 13th District City Hall 200 N. Spring Street, Room 240. Los Angeles, CA 90012 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. 1—95—286 Dear Ms. Goldberg: You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act “the Act”.-/ Since your letter states a general question, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c). 2 ? You have asked if an elected official may form a nonprofit organization. Since you have not provided any facts concerning the nonprofit organization, we cannot provide, specific advice pertaining to this issue. Generally, an elected official is not precluded from forming a nonprofit organization, provided it is formed in a manner consistent with Regulation 18217 (copy enclosed). If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322—5660. Sincerely, - Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel by: bixie Howard Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division Enclosure 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to Councilmember Goldberg's question about whether an elected official may form a nonprofit organization. The FPPC advises that this is generally permissible if done in compliance with Regulation 18217, but cannot provide specific guidance without additional facts.
